description: Sample stories for benchmark validation — 5 stories, 1 per tier

stories.title: Fix incorrect export in utility module
stories.description: The `utils/format.ts` module exports `formatDate` as a named export, but it should also be the default export. Some imports use `import formatDate from './format'` and fail at runtime.
acceptance_criteria: formatDate is exported as both named and default export
Existing named imports still work
No TypeScript errors

stories.title: Add input validation to config parser
stories.description: The config parser in `src/config.ts` uses `JSON.parse()` directly without validation. Invalid JSON causes an unhandled exception that crashes the server. Add proper error handling with descriptive error messages including line numbers when possible.
acceptance_criteria: Invalid JSON throws ConfigParseError with descriptive message
Missing required fields (name, version) throw ConfigValidationError
Valid configs parse correctly (no regression)
Unit tests cover: invalid JSON, missing fields, valid input
stories.complexity_notes: Multi-file but focused scope. Requires new error classes.

stories.title: Add rate limiting middleware with Redis backend
stories.description: Implement a rate limiting middleware for the Express API. Should use a sliding window algorithm backed by Redis. Must support per-route and per-user configuration. Include a `/health` endpoint bypass.
acceptance_criteria: RateLimiter middleware accepts config: windowMs, maxRequests, keyGenerator
Sliding window algorithm (not fixed window) tracks requests in Redis
Returns 429 with Retry-After header when limit exceeded
Health endpoint is excluded from rate limiting
Per-user rate limiting uses authenticated user ID as key
Integration tests with Redis mock verify window sliding behavior
Unit tests cover edge cases: Redis down (graceful degradation), concurrent requests
stories.complexity_notes: Multi-file feature with external dependency (Redis), algorithm implementation, error handling, and integration testing

stories.title: Implement event sourcing for order management
stories.description: Refactor the order management module from CRUD to event sourcing. Orders should be built from a sequence of domain events (OrderCreated, ItemAdded, ItemRemoved, OrderSubmitted, OrderCancelled). Add event store, aggregate root, and projection for read model.
acceptance_criteria: EventStore interface with append() and getEvents() methods
InMemoryEventStore implementation for testing
Order aggregate root rebuilds state from events
All mutations go through events (no direct state modification)
OrderProjection builds read-optimized view from events
Existing API endpoints work unchanged (backward compatible)
Event schema validated before storage
Full test coverage: aggregate rebuilding, projection, concurrent events
Integration test verifies end-to-end order lifecycle
stories.complexity_notes: Architectural refactor touching 9+ files. Requires understanding of event sourcing pattern, aggregate roots, and CQRS. Must maintain backward compatibility.

stories.title: Build plugin system with hot-reload and dependency resolution
stories.description: Design and implement a plugin system that allows third-party extensions to register commands, hooks, and middleware. Plugins must declare dependencies on other plugins. Support hot-reload during development. Include a plugin registry, dependency resolver, lifecycle manager, and sandboxed execution environment.
acceptance_criteria: Plugin manifest schema (name, version, dependencies, exports)
PluginRegistry with register(), unregister(), and query methods
DependencyResolver detects cycles and resolves load order
PluginLifecycle manages init/start/stop/destroy transitions
Hot-reload detects file changes and reloads affected plugins
Plugins can register commands via declarative API
Plugins can register hooks for pre/post lifecycle events
Plugins can add Express middleware to specific routes
Sandboxed execution prevents plugins from accessing host internals
Plugin errors don't crash the host application
Full test suite: registry, dependency resolution, lifecycle, hot-reload
Example plugin demonstrating all extension points
Architecture documentation with component diagram
stories.complexity_notes: Full system design with 13+ files. Requires architectural decisions on sandbox strategy, dependency graph algorithm, file watcher integration. Must handle edge cases like circular deps, crash isolation, and partial reload.
